comment: Parakeet model catalog. Distribution (decided 2026-07-13): the plugin SHIPS with the standard transcription model (Whisper Small Q5) + silero VAD bundled; the standard summarizer (Qwen3.5 2B) auto-downloads on first run (1.3 GB is too heavy for the Plugins Admin zip). All other models are in-panel downloads. This file is the SHIPPED baseline index; the plugin periodically fetches the LIVE index (index_url below, nppPluginList-style) from the Parakeet plugin repo so new models appear without a plugin update — remote entries merge over these by id, shipped file wins on parse failure. Hosting: transcription/VAD/speaker + the two Qwen default summarizers are on our own GitHub models-repo releases (our pinned sha256s); the ADDITIONAL local summarizers (Gemma/Granite/Llama, added catalog_version 6, 2026-07-14) stream directly from their upstream Hugging Face GGUF repos (per-model `repo` + `license` fields) with pinned sha256 — we don't re-host every model. Every download is sha256-verified regardless of host; the NSURLSession downloader follows HF's 302 redirects transparently. Models install to <plugins-config>/Parakeet/models/. (catalog_version 6: removed minicpm5-1b — it declares arch=llama but needs MiniCPM depth-scaling our vendored llama.cpp doesn't apply, so it produced token garbage; replaced with four HF-hosted models verified to generate coherent summaries through our engine.) (catalog_version 7, 2026-07-15: added kind="translation" — a dedicated NMT model, MADLAD-400-3B (Google, T5, Apache-2.0, 419 langs, auto-detects source), plus the strongly-multilingual summarizer GGUFs dual-listed as translators (same file, shared download) so Translate works out-of-the-box on the auto-fetched Qwen 2B. The engine runs decoder-only LLM translators via a chat prompt and T5/NMT models via llama.cpp's encoder-decoder path — it picks by inspecting the model. NLLB-200 (M2M) and Opus-MT (Marian) are NOT listed: our vendored llama.cpp can't load those architectures — they need a separate ONNX/CTranslate2 runtime, a future addition.)

models.name: Silero VAD v5
models.notes: Internal voice-activity model (utterance segmentation). BUNDLED; hidden from the models UI.

models.name: Whisper Small (Q5)
models.notes: BUNDLED with the plugin — the standard transcription model. Strong accuracy incl. non-English; real-time on any modern Mac.

models.name: Whisper Base (Q5)
models.notes: Lightweight option — faster and smaller than Small; weaker outside English.

models.name: Whisper Small
models.notes: Full-precision Small for maximum quality below the large tier.

models.name: Whisper Large v3 Turbo (Q5)
models.notes: Best quality; Apple Silicon recommended.

models.name: Speaker detection — segmentation
models.notes: Part 1/2 of the Speaker detection pack (pyannote segmentation-3.0). With both parts installed, 'Detect speakers' labels voice notes as **Speaker 1/2/…**.

models.name: Speaker detection — voice fingerprints
models.notes: Part 2/2 of the Speaker detection pack (WeSpeaker voice embeddings). Accurate default (ResNet34).

models.name: Speaker detection — CAM++ (faster)
models.notes: Alternative voice-fingerprint model (3D-Speaker CAM++) — ~2x faster than ResNet34 for speaker detection (its TDNN architecture is cheaper than ResNet34's 2D convolutions). Pick it in a Speaker model's Settings… once installed. Streams from Hugging Face.

models.name: Qwen3.5 2B (Q4)
models.notes: STANDARD summarizer (Phase 2): Apache-2.0, 100+ languages (matches Whisper), 262k context = whole meeting transcripts without chunking. Needs current llama.cpp (Gated DeltaNet). Auto-downloads in the background on first run (too big to bundle in the plugin zip).

models.name: Qwen3.5 0.8B (Q4)
models.notes: Low-RAM summarizer (Intel Macs / 8 GB machines). Same architecture and language coverage as the 2B.

models.name: Gemma 3 1B (Q4)
models.notes: Smallest local summarizer — Google Gemma 3, 140+ languages, exceptional quality-per-byte; comfortable on 8 GB Macs. Streams from Hugging Face (ggml-org GGUF).

models.name: Granite 3.3 2B (Q4)
models.notes: IBM Granite 3.3 — fully Apache-2.0 (no usage restrictions), tuned for enterprise summarization/RAG. Streams from Hugging Face (ibm-granite GGUF).

models.name: Llama 3.2 3B (Q4)
models.notes: Meta Llama 3.2 — a widely-trusted general summarizer, strong in English. Streams from Hugging Face (bartowski GGUF).

models.name: Gemma 3 4B (Q4)
models.notes: Highest-quality local option below the cloud tier — Google Gemma 3 4B, 140+ languages, the most detailed on-device notes. Needs more RAM. Streams from Hugging Face (ggml-org GGUF).

models.name: NLLB-200 (600M)
models.notes: DEDICATED translator — Meta's NLLB-200 (distilled 600M, int8) via CTranslate2. One compact model covering 200 languages with the best translation quality of the local options, and fast batched decoding (a short note in a few seconds). Purpose-built for machine translation.

models.name: MADLAD-400 3B (Q4)
models.notes: DEDICATED translator — Google MADLAD-400 (T5), 419 languages in one model, auto-detects the source language. Apache-2.0, purpose-built for machine translation. Streams from Hugging Face.

models.name: Qwen3.5 2B (Q4)
models.notes: STANDARD translator — the same Qwen3.5 2B that powers summaries (Apache-2.0, 100+ languages), prompted to translate into your target language. Shares its download with the summarizer, so Translate works as soon as the summarizer has been fetched.

models.name: Qwen3.5 0.8B (Q4)
models.notes: Low-RAM translator (Intel Macs / 8 GB machines). Same Qwen3.5 architecture and language coverage as the 2B; shares its download with the summarizer.

models.name: Gemma 3 1B (Q4)
models.notes: Smallest LLM translator — Google Gemma 3 1B, 140+ languages, great quality-per-byte. Shares its download with the summarizer.

models.name: Gemma 3 4B (Q4)
models.notes: Highest-quality LLM translator — Google Gemma 3 4B, 140+ languages. Needs more RAM. Shares its download with the summarizer.

models.comment: TOMBSTONE (retired 2026-07-14). MiniCPM5's GGUF declares arch=llama but needs MiniCPM depth-scaling our vendored llama.cpp doesn't apply, so it emitted token garbage. This file-less hidden entry retracts the id from any catalog layer that still lists it (e.g. a bundled v5 catalog on an already-installed plugin). Do not re-use this id.

models.name: Claude (your account)
models.notes: Summarize with your own Claude account. Settings… → add an Anthropic API key (pay-as-you-go) or use the Claude Code CLI (Pro/Max subscription). Your note is sent to Anthropic — not on-device.

models.name: ChatGPT (your account)
models.notes: Summarize with your own OpenAI account. Settings… → add an OpenAI API key (pay-as-you-go) or use the Codex CLI (ChatGPT subscription). Your note is sent to OpenAI — not on-device.

models.name: Grok (your account)
models.notes: Summarize with your own xAI account. Settings… → add an xAI API key (pay-as-you-go; Grok has no local CLI). Your note is sent to xAI — not on-device.

models.name: Claude (your account)
models.notes: Translate with your own Claude account — fast and high quality, runs on Anthropic's servers (no local compute). Uses the SAME account as the Claude Summarizer (set it up once in Settings…). Your note is sent to Anthropic.

models.name: ChatGPT (your account)
models.notes: Translate with your own OpenAI account — fast and high quality, runs on OpenAI's servers (no local compute). Uses the SAME account as the ChatGPT Summarizer (set it up once in Settings…). Your note is sent to OpenAI.

models.name: Grok (your account)
models.notes: Translate with your own xAI account — fast, runs on xAI's servers (no local compute). Uses the SAME account as the Grok Summarizer (add an xAI API key once in Settings…). Your note is sent to xAI.
